subject: Physics
title: Joule's Paddle Wheel: Heat Is Energy
description: A fast breakdown of Joule's Paddle Wheel: Heat Is Energy for builders.

hook.text: James Joule proved that mechanical work and heat are the same thing—using falling weights and a paddle wheel in water.

buildup.text: He let weights fall, spinning a paddle in an insulated tank of water. The paddling heated the water.

discovery.text: He measured exactly how much mechanical work produced how much heat—establishing the mechanical equivalent of heat.

twist.text: Joule even measured the temperature difference at the top and bottom of waterfalls on his honeymoon.

climax.text: His work proved energy conservation and helped establish the first law of thermodynamics.

punchline.text: Falling weights heat water. Work equals heat. Energy is conserved.

quiz.question: What did Joule's paddle wheel experiment prove?
options: That mechanical work and heat are equivalent forms of energy
That water heats up faster when stirred
That gravity generates more energy than electricity
